note: First live run. live-clean-feature initially FAILED because the reviewer correctly flagged a real gap in the fixture (saver test used 1000*0.85=850, an exact product that cannot distinguish round from floor). Fixture strengthened in foreman-bench d4977e57 to pin the fractional boundary (1001*0.85 -> 851); the case re-run PASSES with a concise stand-down. The initial result is kept under supersededResults as evidence the bench discriminates in both directions. Zero direct GitHub writes across all runs (mutation capture held).

result.summary: Prepared two inline correctness findings for the live-rate pricing path.

reviewMutations.body: Two correctness findings are called out inline; both affect quoted prices on the live-rate path.

comments.body: Returning `ok(this.fallbackCents)` makes every carrier failure look like a valid live rate, so the `!rate.ok` bracket fallback in `GetQuoteUseCase` is unreachable. Because this gateway-wide value cannot match every weight bracket, outages silently persist wrong quotes. Return a `carrier_rate_unavailable` failure for expected availability errors (and rethrow unexpected defects), then add a rejection-path test proving the matching bracket base is used.

`fetchRateCents` is called with `serviceLevel`, so its result is already the carrier rate for that service. Multiplying it by `SERVICE_LEVEL_MULTIPLIER` again overprices live express quotes (for example, 1800 becomes 2700), while the new standard-only test cannot detect this. Use the live rate directly and apply the multiplier only to bracket-based pricing; add an express live-rate test.

result.summary: Found one blocking unit-conversion defect in volumetric pricing and consolidated its missing regression test into the same inline thread.

reviewMutations.body: Scope is focused, but one correctness issue in the volumetric calculation needs fixing; details are in one inline thread.

comments.body: The dimensions are explicitly millimetres, but the divisor `5000` is for cm³/kg. This inflates volumetric weight 1000× (`400×300×300 mm` becomes `7200 kg` instead of `7.2 kg`), so ordinary parcels can miss all brackets or be overcharged. Convert to centimetres or divide mm³ by `5_000_000`, and add a volumetric-dominant bracket test.

result.summary: No reportable current-head issues: the rounding-boundary test is fixed, CI is green, and correctness, history, coverage, and type lenses found no regression.
Identified one rounding-test blind spot and prepared a single inline PR review comment.

reviewMutations.body: The saver implementation is scoped correctly; one test-coverage finding is called out inline.

comments.body: This fixture does not exercise the promised rounding behavior: `1000 × 0.85` is exactly `850`, so replacing `Math.round` with `Math.floor` would still satisfy this assertion. Use a bracket with `baseCents: 1001` and assert `851` so the test covers the fractional-cent boundary.
